cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the process of organizing and interpreting sensory information, enabling us to recognize meaningful objects and events

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: analysis that begins with the sensory receptors and works up to the brain's integration of sensory information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: information processing guided by higher-level mental processes, as when we construct perceptions drawing on our experience and expectations

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A predisposition or readiness to perceive something in a particular way

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the tendency to complete figures that are incomplete

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the organization of the visual field into objects that stand out from their surroundings.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the grouping of objects or items that are close to each other.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the tendency to perceive things that look similar to each other as being part of the same group

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Focusing awareness on a narrowed range of stimuli or events

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The ability to focus on only one stimulus from among all sensory input

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Ability to concentrate on one voice amongst a crowd

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Failing to see visible objects when our attention is directed elsewhere

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Failing to notice changes in the environment; a form of inattentional blindness

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Cues of depth perception that arise from the fact that people have two eyes

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a binocular cue for perceiving depth by comparing images from the retinas in the two eyes, the brain computes distance — the greater the difference between the two images, the closer the object.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A binocular cue for perceiving depth; the extent to which the eyes cross when looking at an object

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Aspects of a scene that yield information about depth when viewed with only one eye

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a monocular cue for perceiving depth; hazy objects are farther away than sharp, clear objects

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a monocular cue for perceiving depth; the smaller image is farther away

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a monocular cue for perceiving depth; a gradual change from a coarse distinct texture to a fine, indistinct texture signals with increasing distance.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A monocular cue for perceiving depth; the more parallel lines converge, the greater their perceived distance.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: monocular cue for depth perception; if one object partially blocks our view of another object, we perceive it as closer

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An illusion of movement perception that occurs when stimuli in different locations are flashed one after another with the proper timing.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A mental image or best example of a category

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a concept or framework that organizes and interprets information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Interpreting new experiences in terms of our existing schemas

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Adapting our current understandings (schemas) to incorporate new information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A methodical, logical rule or procedure that guarantees solving a particular problem.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a simple thinking strategy that often allows us to make judgments and solve problems efficiently; usually speedier but also more error-prone than algorithms

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Judging the likelihood of things in terms of how well they seem to represent, or match, particular prototypes; may lead us to ignore other relevant information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: estimating the likelihood of events based on their availability in memory; if instances come readily to mind (perhaps because of their vividness), we presume such events are common

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a tendency to approach a problem in one particular way, often a way that has been successful in the past

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the activation, often unconsciously, of certain associations, thus predisposing one's perception, memory, or response

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The way an issue is posed and its effect on decisionmaking

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The belief that the odds of a chance event increase if the event hasn't occurred recently

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a framing effect in which people make decisions about a current situation based on what they have previously invested in the situation

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: higher-order, complex cognitive processes, including thinking, planning, and problem solving

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the ability to produce novel and valuable ideas

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: expanding the number of possible problem solutions; creative thinking that diverges in different directions

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: narrows the available problem solutions to determine the single best solution

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the tendency to think of things only in terms of their usual functions; an impediment to problem solving

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the processing of information into the memory system

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the retention of encoded information over time

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the process of getting information out of memory storage

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memory of facts and experiences that one can consciously know and "declare"

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: remembering to do something at some future time

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the collection of past personal experiences that occurred at a particular time and place

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a network of associated facts and concepts that make up our general knowledge of the world

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Memories we don't deliberately remember or reflect on consciously

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: T-he gradual acquisition of skills as a result of practice, or "knowing how" to do things

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An increase in a cell's firing potential after brief, rapid stimulation. Believed to be a neural basis for learning and memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An explanation that sees short-term memory as an active store holding several pieces of information simultaneously.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A newer understanding of short-term memory that involves conscious, active processing of incoming auditory and visual-spatial information, and of information retrieved from long-term memory.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The part of working memory that is responsible for monitoring and directing attention and other mental resources.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the part of working memory that holds and processes verbal and auditory information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A component of working memory where we create mental images to remember visual information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the relatively permanent and limitless storehouse of the memory system. Includes knowledge, skills, and experiences.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Explanation of memory that sees information flowing through a series of storage systems

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a momentary sensory memory of auditory stimuli

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a momentary sensory memory of visual stimuli

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: unconscious encoding of incidental information, such as space, time, and frequency, and of well-learned information, such as word meanings

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: encoding that requires attention and conscious effort

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: model of memory that assumes information that is more "deeply processed," or processed according to its meaning rather than just the sound or physical characteristics of the word or words, will be remembered more efficiently and for a longer period of time

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Encoding based on surface features (what something looks or sounds like)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: encoding based on an event's meaning as well as connections between the new event and past experience

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Relatively shallow processing that emphasizes the physical structure of the stimulus

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Encoding information according to the sound of the word used to identify it.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the encoding of meaning, including the meaning of words

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Method of improving memory by associating new information with previously learned information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A mnemonic technique that involves associating items on a list with a sequence of familiar physical locations

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Organizing items into familiar, manageable units

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: networks of associated memories that have features in common with each other

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Complex information broken down into broad concepts and further subdivided into categories and subcategories

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the tendency for distributed study or practice to yield better long-term retention than is achieved through massed study or practice

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a practice schedule in which studying continues for long periods, without interruption

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Spacing the study of material to be remembered by including breaks between study periods

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Our tendency to recall best the last (a recency effect) and first items (a primacy effect) in a list

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: tendency to remember words at the beginning of a list especially well.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: tendency to remember words at the end of a list especially well

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the immediate, very brief recording of sensory information in the memory system

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: activated memory that holds a few items briefly, such as the seven digits of a phone number while dialing, before the information is stored or forgotten

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A system for remembering involving repeating information to oneself without attempting to find meaning in it

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a method of transferring information from short term memory into long term memory by making that information meaningful in some way

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Ability to recall information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The memory for events and facts related to one's personal life story

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Loss of memory from the point of some injury or trauma backwards, or loss of memory for the past

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the inability to transfer new information from the short-term store into the long-term store

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a progressive and irreversible brain disorder characterized by gradual deterioration of memory, reasoning, language, and, finally, physical functioning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The inability to remember events from early childhood

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A measure of memory in which the person must retrieve information learned earlier, as on a fill-in-the-blank test.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the ability to match a piece of information or a stimulus to a stored image or fact

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Stimuli that are used to bring a memory to consciousness or into behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The theory that information learned in a particular situation or place is better remembered when in that same situation or place.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the tendency to recall experiences that are consistent with one's current good or bad mood

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the phenomenon through which memory retrieval is most efficient when an individual is in the same state of consciousness as they were when the memory was formed

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: enhanced memory after retrieving, rather than simply rereading, information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Awareness and understanding of one's own thought processes.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A graph showing retention and forgetting over time.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the inability to recall specific information because of insufficient encoding of the information for storage in long-term memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the disruptive effect of prior learning on the recall of new information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the disruptive effect of new learning on the recall of old information

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the temporary inability to remember something you know, accompanied by a feeling that it's just out of reach

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: in psychoanalytic theory, the basic defense mechanism that banishes from consciousness anxiety-arousing thoughts, feelings, and memories

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: incorporating misleading information into one's memory of an event

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: attributing to the wrong source an event we have experienced, heard about, read about, or imagined

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the act of remembering construed in terms of making inferences about the past, based on what is currently known and accessible

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the gradual, physical process of converting new long-term memories to stable, enduring memory codes

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a memory phenomenon in which vividly imagining an event markedly increases confidence that the event actually occurred

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: mental quality consisting of the ability to learn from experience, solve problems, and use knowledge to adapt to new situations

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a single intelligence factor that underlies specific mental abilities and is therefore measured by every task on an intelligence test

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: defined originally as the ratio of mental age to chronological age multiplied by 100; an early measure of intelligence

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A measure equal to one's chronological age times the percentage score on an IQ test

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Age as measured in years from date of birth

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defining meaningful scores by comparison with the performance of a pretested group

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the extent to which a test measures or predicts what it is supposed to

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the extent to which variables measure what they are supposed to measure

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The success with which a test predicts the behavior it is designed to predict; it is assessed by computing the correlation between test scores and the criterion behavior.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The extent to which a test yields consistent results.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a method for determining the reliability of a test by comparing a test taker's scores on the same test taken on separate occasions

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A measure of reliability in which a test is split into two parts and an individual's scores on both halves are compared.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: a self-confirming concern that one will be evaluated based on a negative stereotype

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: awareness of positive expectations can actually improve performance on tasks

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the worldwide phenomenon that shows intelligence test performance has been increasing over the years

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: tests designed to assess what a person has learned.

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: tests designed to predict a person's future performance; aptitude is the capacity to learn

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the idea that we have a set amount of an ability that cannot change

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: the idea that our abilities are malleable qualities that we can cultivate and grow
